op.text: Priya signed lease for new ceramics studio and has keys as of November 2025; studio described as smelling 'like wet clay and fresh paint'; Dana has a shelf there for her own ceramics work

[2025-11-22] At Priya's new studio on a Tuesday (shortly before 2025-11-22), Priya asked offhand whether Dana was going to go for the director job in spring as she'd said. Dana said: 'Honestly? I'm not sure I want the director track at all anymore. I'm not sure I ever did.' Dana described having to sit down because she thinks it's true. She said: 'Deferring the promotion, I could file under grief and timing. But "maybe I never wanted it" — that's saying the last fifteen years of climbing might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine.' Dana described the realization as 'uncomfortable and correct, which is usually the combination that means something real.' She said: 'I'm not going to blow up my career over a Tuesday epiphany at a pottery wheel — I have a mortgage and I actually like being competent at the work. But the story where getting to director was the point of me? I don't think I believe it anymore. I think I stopped believing it and only just noticed.' (2025-11-22)

op.note: Session 2025-11-22: Dana articulated that she may never have actually wanted the director track — that fifteen years of climbing 'might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine.' This suggests the historical self-definition through work ambition may have been partly externally driven rather than intrinsic. Belief bel-53 describes ambition as 'my whole spine' and 'how I've always known I'm okay' — Dana's new framing questions whether that spine was ever truly hers. Noting as mismatch rather than superseding because this is early articulation of a realization, not yet a settled new belief.
